rulings.Case Title: TONY GARNICKI ET AL VS. ANCHOR LOANS LP, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 26, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND WRIT OF ATTACHMENT will be CONTINUED to June 5, 2023 per the stipulation of the parties if a hard copy of the stipulation and proposed order is presented for execution no later than 9:00 a.m. on May 26, 2023. If no copy of the stipulation and proposed order is received by 9:00 a.m. on May 26, 2023, the motion will be taken off calendar. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LUMINOUS ION LLC VS. VICKI CHIAO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Sever Cross Complaint Of Eastwood Development Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 26, 2023 line 2. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SEVER CROSS-COMPLAINT OF EASTWOOD DEVELOPMENT INC. Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on May 26, 2023 Eastwood Development Inc to file a declaration stating (1) each cross-defendant; (2) the date each cross-defendant was served; (3) the date proof of service for each cross-defendant was filed; (4) if a cross-defendant has not been served as of May 26, 2023 state the reason for failure to serve and an expected date of service; (5) if a cross-defendant had been served, but no proof of service was filed, explain the reason for failure to file a proof of service; (6) for each served cross-defendant state the date the response is due; (7) for each cross-defendant, if any, that failed to timely respond, state the date the default was requested; (8) for each cross-defendant, if any, that failed to timely respond and no default has been requested, state the reason for failure to request a default. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FELLOWSHIP MANOR OF BETHEL A.M.E. CHURCH VS. ETHEL BRIGHAM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 26, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT ETHEL BRIGHAM'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT is GRANTED. The Court accepts Defendant's uncontroverted declaration that she was not aware of the substitute service, which resulted in lack of actual notice and excusable neglect leading to failure to timely respond to the complaint. Defendant shall file an answer no later than May 26, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
